title: Cyclosorus × gogoii
intro: Cyclosorus × gogoii, also known as the Gogo fern, is a hybrid plant species within the Blechnaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Pacific Island cultures, particularly in Fiji, for its medicinal properties, exhibiting health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × gogoii include flavonoids, phenolic compounds, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Fijian medicine, this plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to make teas and infusions for various health purposes.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × gogoii is a nothospecies, specifically a hybrid of two different species within the genus Cyclosorus. It is classified as a member of the family Thelypteridaceae, which includes a diverse group of ferns. This hybrid fern is often found in certain regions of New Zealand. The classification of Cyclosorus × gogoii is further broken down into its botanical classification: Cyclosorus × gogoii, a nothospecies within the genus Cyclosorus.
